name: Homemade Pita Bread
method: In a large mixing basin, whisk together warm water, yeast, and
sugar until completely dissolved. 12 cup flour, stirred in and set
aside for 15 minutes, or until mixture foams.
Stir in the oil, salt, and 2 cups flour (reserving 12 cups) until a
shaggy mass forms using a wooden spoon. Dust a clean surface
with part of the saved flour and knead the dough for about 7
minutes, or until smooth and elastic, adding additional reserved
flour if the dough is too sticky. Soft and wet dough is ideal. (If
you need a break from kneading, cover the dough and set it
aside for 10 minutes.)
Cover dough with plastic wrap in a clean big mixing basin.
Allow to rise in a warm area for 1 hour or until the dough has
doubled in size.
Preheat your oven to 500° with a pizza stone or a big cast-iron
pan inside. Dust a clean surface lightly with flour. Turn the
dough out onto the surface after punching it down. Divide the
dough into eight pieces and shape each piece into a ball. Allow
10 minutes to rest after covering with a towel or plastic wrap.
Working one at a time, roll each round into a 14"-thick, 8"-wide
circle, sprinkling additional flour on the dough if it starts to
stick.
As fast as possible, open your oven and arrange as many pitas as
will fit without touching on the pan or stone. Allow 3 to 5
minutes for an air pocket to form and the pita to turn golden
brown. Continue with the remaining rounds.
To keep cooked pitas warm, cover them with a clean kitchen
towel.
